compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The plan-review gate (pass 1 finding discovery, pass 2 validation/impact, pass 4 coaching) measures plan quality against packaged criteria. We want to assess it against the state of the art on AI task decomposition and produce recommendations that improve its ability to detect decomposition flaws in plans without raising false positives.

## What
1. Deep research on AI task decomposition: recent data-driven research (papers with data + code) and OSS community convergence observed in skills/prompts that decompose software-engineering tasks.
2. Synthesize with the two operator-supplied reports (~/Downloads/ai-task-decomposition-report.md and "AI Task Decomposition Best Practices.md") into a cited state-of-the-art report at docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, with quoted prompts/code and specific data; commit it.
3. Map the report's practices onto what plan review measures today (criteria registry, DET floor, LLM criteria), respecting the strict pass 1 / pass 2 / pass 4 separation of concerns.
4. Produce recommendations, each with (a) a falsifiable experiment to validate improved flaw detection without added false positives, and (b) an assessment against this repo's actual plan-review history (attestations/review sidecars) for whether it would have generated valid findings or false positives.
5. Quantify the "ticket edited after entering in_progress" signal from store history as a proxy for plan-review misses, and use it to ground the recommendations.

## Scope / Files
- New file: docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md (the committed deliverable; recommendations included as a section).
- Read-only analysis of: src/rebar/llm/ (plan-review passes and criteria registry), src/rebar/plan_review/, .rebar/prompts/, .rebar/criteria_routing.json, docs/plan-review-gate.md, and the tickets store history (events on the tickets branch).
- Non-goals: NO code changes to the gate in this task — recommendations only; implementation is follow-up work to be filed as discovered_from tickets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A report exists at docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md with citations, quoted code/prompts from OSS projects, and specific quantitative data, synthesizing independent research with the two Downloads reports.
- [ ] The report is committed on main with a rebar-ticket trailer referencing this ticket.
- [ ] The report contains a gap analysis mapping decomposition best practices to what plan-review passes 1, 2, and 4 each measure, keeping each recommendation scoped to the correct pass.
- [ ] At least 5 recommendations, each with a falsifiable experiment (metric + pass/fail condition) and a false-positive assessment grounded in actual plan-review history from this repo's store.
- [ ] The post-claim ticket-edit signal is quantified from store history and cited in at least one recommendation.

## Verification
- `test -f docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md`
- `git log -1 --format=%B -- docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md | grep -i rebar-ticket`
compiled_state.title: Task-decomposition SOTA report + plan-review gap analysis and recommendations

verify_commands.dd_text: Report file exists and is committed with ticket trailer
